Coagulation

Test27-JUL-2024Reference RangeUnit
Prothrombin time (PT)10.3 L11.0 - 13.5s

aPTT in Blood by Coagulation assay38.5 H24 - 32s

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild normocytic anemia, severe hyperbilirubinemia, elevated creatinine indicating significant renal impairment, and metabolic acidosis (low pH, low bicarbonate, low O2 saturation). Glucose is slightly low. Coagulation tests show prolonged aPTT. These findings suggest acute or chronic kidney dysfunction with possible hepatic involvement and impaired oxygenation. Immediate clinical evaluation is recommended.
